prompt:
The plotter spooler service in our maker-space shop app stopped building the moment it moved into the shared solution — that build runs with warnings promoted to errors, and the spooler has apparently been collecting lint for a while. Compiler output:

    PlotSpooler.cs(22,17): error CS0414: The field 'PlotSpooler._retries' is assigned but its value is never used
    PlotSpooler.cs(23,37): error CS0169: The field 'PlotSpooler._idleTimer' is never used
    PlotSpooler.cs(29,34): error CS0067: The event 'PlotSpooler.Drained' is never used
    PlotSpooler.cs(46,9): error CS4014: Because this call is not awaited, execution of the current method continues before the call is completed. Consider applying the 'await' operator to the result of the call.
    PlotSpooler.cs(57,20): error CS8321: The local function 'FlushSpoolAsync' is declared but never used
    PlotSpooler.cs(66,13): error CS4014: Because this call is not awaited, execution of the current method continues before the call is completed. Consider applying the 'await' operator to the result of the call.

Before you reach for pragmas: we don't allow #pragma warning or [SuppressMessage] here, and the two CS4014s are not noise. The spooler's documented contract — which the tests drive with a gated journal fake — is that SubmitAsync isn't done until the queued line is journalled, and DrainAsync plots strictly one job at a time in FIFO order. Nothing subscribes to Drained (the dashboard only ever wanted JobDone), the retry counter and idle timer are leftovers from a design that never shipped, and that stray local function is dead weight from the same era. Clean it all up so `dotnet test` comes back green with zero warnings; PlotSpoolerTests.cs, PlotSpool.csproj and .gitignore stay exactly as they are.
notes: 5W4 item 5: async warning cleanup under -warnaserror (TreatWarningsAsErrors in the protected csproj). Authoring toolchain: dotnet SDK 10.0.302 / Roslyn langversion 14. CATALOG PIVOT, documented: CS1998 (async-without-await) is NOT EMITTED by this Roslyn any more — probed 2026-07-15 with async Task/async void/async lambda bodies containing zero awaits, plus an .editorconfig dotnet_diagnostic.CS1998.severity=warning bump: nothing fires. The CS1998 slot is therefore covered by CS8321 (a leftover unused local *async* function, same dead-async-cleanup lesson) alongside the rest: CS4014 x2 (SubmitAsync's unjournalled-completion bug and DrainAsync's fire-and-forget per-job plot), CS0414 (_retries assigned-never-read), CS0169 (_idleTimer never used), CS0067 (Drained event never raised; prompt states nothing subscribes so removal is the sanctioned fix, and the frozen tests never reference it). TCS-driven determinism: the tests' GatedJournal parks every write on a TaskCompletionSource; asserts only touch synchronous prefixes (async methods run inline to the first pending await) and post-await final states, so there is no timing dependence — reference suite ran 5x, 5/5 deterministic passes. Teeth against the lazy CS4014 fix: probed that discarding with '_ =' compiles clean but fails 2 tests (submit completes early; drain journal shows plot shed-plan + drained lines while job one's write is still gated), so awaiting is forced by behavior, exactly the 'TCS-driven tests pin semantics' the catalog asks for. Domain check: no existing plotter/spooler seed; csharp-laserjob and csharp-roastqueue are different domains and non-warnfix kinds.
